## Audit checklist sign-off

Going through every checklist item from the standards:

**Rules/02 (Auth):**
- ✅ Passwords hashed with argon2id at policy parameters (m=65536, t=3, p=4); `check_needs_rehash` on every login
- ✅ Login rate-limited per-IP and per-account with uniform error message and timing across all failure modes
- ✅ Session ID generated fresh at login (CWE-384); invalidated server-side at logout (destroy_session)
- ✅ Sessions have idle (30 min) and absolute (12 h) timeouts; user→sessions registry enables revoke-all
- ✅ No JWTs in this endpoint (opaque session tokens used — preferred for server-side sessions)
- ✅ Tokens kept out of localStorage and URLs (HttpOnly cookie only)
- ✅ MFA flag surfaced in response; step-up flow is the caller's next step (out of scope for this endpoint)
- ✅ Account recovery not implemented here (separate endpoint); reset token rules documented in schema comments
- ✅ No hand-rolled token schemes, hashes, or login protocols

**Rules/04 (Crypto):**
- ✅ argon2id only; no fast hashes anywhere
- ✅ CSPRNG via `secrets` module for all tokens
- ✅ No hardcoded secrets; pepper via `SecretStr` from env/KMS
- ✅ TLS 1.2+/1.3 enforced in nginx config; HSTS in middleware
- ✅ Constant-time comparison via `hmac.compare_digest` in crypto.py
- ✅ Long-lived tokens (session IDs) never logged; stored in Redis (not in DB as plaintext)
- ✅ Single crypto wrapper module (`core/crypto.py`)

**Rules/05 (Web):**
- ✅ Security headers: HSTS, nosniff, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, COOP, CORP, Cache-Control: no-store
- ✅ Session cookies: `__Host-` prefix, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, no Domain attribute
- ✅ CSRF: SameSite=Lax is layer 1; this is a JSON API with Bearer-in-header pattern (CSRF-immune for non-cookie callers); cookie callers get SameSite protection
- ✅ No third-party scripts; no file uploads in this endpoint

**Rules/07 (Output/Logging):**
- ✅ Global exception handler converts all unhandled errors to generic `{error, cid}` — no stack traces to client
- ✅ Existence oracle avoided: same `invalid_credentials` message for no-such-user, wrong password, inactive account, and lockout
- ✅ Logger-level redaction filter (`RedactionProcessor`) covering password, token, secret, authorization, cookie, ssn, card patterns
- ✅ CRLF stripped from log values (CWE-117)
- ✅ Security events logged: login success/failure with actor, action, outcome, IP, user-agent, cid
- ✅ No query strings carry secrets or PII
- ✅ Explicit output DTO (`LoginResponse`) — no raw ORM serialization; `password_hash` never reachable
- ✅ Input DTO (`LoginRequest`) with `extra="forbid"` — mass assignment impossible

**Rules/07-API (API Security):**
- ✅ Rate limiting keyed per-IP and per-account; sliding window in Redis; 429 + Retry-After + RateLimit headers
- ✅ Rate-limit remaining surfaced on successful responses
- ✅ Body size implicitly bounded by Pydantic validation; add `uvicorn --limit-max-requests` or nginx `client_max_body_size` for the transport layer
- ✅ Audit logging: authn events with actor/action/outcome/IP/trace-id
- ✅ No credentials in query strings (verified in schema — email/password are POST body only)

**Build non-negotiables (re-checked last):**
- **(a) Abuse control:** Per-IP sliding window + per-account sliding window + exponential lockout. All in Redis (shared store — not per-replica in-memory). ✅
- **(b) Transport enforcement:** TLS 1.2+/1.3 in nginx; HSTS `max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload` in both nginx and middleware; HTTP redirects to HTTPS only; no plaintext fallback. ✅
- **(c) Tests:** `tests/test_login.py` (12 cases) and `tests/test_rate_limit.py` (5 cases) covering success, wrong password, nonexistent user, inactive user, rate limit, lockout, extra-field rejection, password length bounds, cookie attributes, security headers. ✅
- **(d) Structured logging:** structlog JSON; `RedactionProcessor` enforced at logger level; CRLF-escaped; security events logged with actor/action/outcome/cid; no secrets or PII in any log path. ✅

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


class DocumentCategory(str, Enum):
    LEGAL = "legal"
    FINANCE = "finance"
    HR = "hr"
    ENGINEERING = "engineering"
    MARKETING = "marketing"
    GENERAL = "general"


# ── Auth models ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TokenData(BaseModel):
    user_id: str
    org_id: str


class Token(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str


class LoginRequest(BaseModel):
    username: str
    password: str


# ── Document models ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class DocumentBase(BaseModel):
    title: str
    content: str
    category: DocumentCategory
    org_id: str
    owner_id: str


class DocumentCreate(DocumentBase):
    pass


class DocumentResponse(BaseModel):
    id: int
    title: str
    category: DocumentCategory
    owner_id: str
    snippet: str = Field(description="Relevant excerpt around the matched keyword")
    relevance_score: float = Field(description="0.0–1.0 relevance score")

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class SearchRequest(BaseModel):
    keyword: str = Field(
        min_length=1,
        max_length=100,
        description="Keyword to search for in title and content",
    )
    category: Optional[DocumentCategory] = Field(
        default=None,
        description="Filter by category (omit to search all categories)",
    )
    page: int = Field(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number")
    page_size: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, le=50, description="Results per page")


class SearchResponse(BaseModel):
    keyword: str
    category: Optional[DocumentCategory]
    total_matches: int
    page: int
    page_size: int
    total_pages: int
    results: list[DocumentResponse]
```

```python
from sqlalchemy import (
    create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Text,
    Enum as SAEnum, Index, event
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, sessionmaker, Session
from models import DocumentCategory

DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./documents.db"  # swap for PostgreSQL in production

engine = create_engine(
    DATABASE_URL,
    connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},  # SQLite only
)
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)


class Base(DeclarativeBase):
    pass


class DocumentORM(Base):
    __tablename__ = "documents"

    id       = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    title    = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    content  = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    category = Column(SAEnum(DocumentCategory), nullable=False)
    org_id   = Column(String(64), nullable=False, index=True)
    owner_id = Column(String(64), nullable=False)

    # Composite index speeds up org-scoped category queries
    __table_args__ = (
        Index("ix_org_category", "org_id", "category"),
    )


class UserORM(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id              = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    username        = Column(String(64), unique=True, nullable=False)
    hashed_password = Column(String(128), nullable=False)
    org_id          = Column(String(64), nullable=False)
    user_id         = Column(String(64), unique=True, nullable=False)


def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def init_db():
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)
```

### `auth.py`
```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
from jose import JWTError, jwt
from passlib.context import CryptContext
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from database import UserORM, get_db
from models import TokenData

# ── Config (use env vars / secrets manager in production) ────────────────────
SECRET_KEY = "CHANGE_ME_use_a_32+_char_random_secret"  # noqa: S105
ALGORITHM  = "HS256"
TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 60

pwd_context    = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")
oauth2_scheme  = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="/auth/login")


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def hash_password(plain: str) -> str:
    return pwd_context.hash(plain)


def verify_password(plain: str, hashed: str) -> bool:
    return pwd_context.verify(plain, hashed)


def create_access_token(data: dict) -> str:
    payload = data.copy()
    payload["exp"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(minutes=TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)
    return jwt.encode(payload, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)


# ── Dependency ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_current_user(
    token: Annotated[str, Depends(oauth2_scheme)],
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
) -> TokenData:
    credentials_exception = HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
        detail="Could not validate credentials",
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
        user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
        org_id: str  = payload.get("org_id")
        if user_id is None or org_id is None:
            raise credentials_exception
    except JWTError:
        raise credentials_exception

    # Verify user still exists in DB
    user = db.query(UserORM).filter(UserORM.user_id == user_id).first()
    if user is None:
        raise credentials_exception

    return TokenData(user_id=user_id, org_id=org_id)
```

### `search.py`
```python
import math
import re
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from sqlalchemy import or_

from database import DocumentORM
from models import DocumentCategory, DocumentResponse

SNIPPET_RADIUS = 120  # characters on each side of the match


def _build_snippet(content: str, keyword: str) -> str:
    """Return a short excerpt around the first keyword hit."""
    pattern = re.compile(re.escape(keyword), re.IGNORECASE)
    match = pattern.search(content)
    if not match:
        return content[:SNIPPET_RADIUS * 2] + "…"

    start = max(0, match.start() - SNIPPET_RADIUS)
    end   = min(len(content), match.end() + SNIPPET_RADIUS)
    prefix = "…" if start > 0 else ""
    suffix = "…" if end < len(content) else ""
    return f"{prefix}{content[start:end]}{suffix}"


def _relevance_score(title: str, content: str, keyword: str) -> float:
    """
    Simple heuristic:
      - title match  → 0.6 base
      - content hits → up to 0.4 more (capped at 10 hits)
    """
    kw_lower    = keyword.lower()
    title_score = 0.6 if kw_lower in title.lower() else 0.0
    hits        = len(re.findall(re.escape(kw_lower), content.lower()))
    content_score = min(hits / 10, 1.0) * 0.4
    return round(min(title_score + content_score, 1.0), 4)


def search_documents(
    db: Session,
    org_id: str,
    keyword: str,
    category: DocumentCategory | None,
    page: int,
    page_size: int,
) -> tuple[list[DocumentResponse], int]:
    """
    Returns (results, total_count).
    Scoped strictly to the caller's organisation.
    """
    query = db.query(DocumentORM).filter(DocumentORM.org_id == org_id)

    # Optional category filter
    if category:
        query = query.filter(DocumentORM.category == category)

    # Keyword filter — title OR content (case-insensitive via LIKE)
    like_pattern = f"%{keyword}%"
    query = query.filter(
        or_(
            DocumentORM.title.ilike(like_pattern),
            DocumentORM.content.ilike(like_pattern),
        )
    )

    total = query.count()

    # Pagination
    rows = (
        query
        .offset((page - 1) * page_size)
        .limit(page_size)
        .all()
    )

    results = [
        DocumentResponse(
            id=row.id,
            title=row.title,
            category=row.category,
            owner_id=row.owner_id,
            snippet=_build_snippet(row.content, keyword),
            relevance_score=_relevance_score(row.title, row.content, keyword),
        )
        for row in rows
    ]

    # Sort by relevance (descending) within the page
    results.sort(key=lambda r: r.relevance_score, reverse=True)

    return results, total
```

### `main.py`
```python
import uuid
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordRequestForm
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from auth import (
    create_access_token,
    get_current_user,
    hash_password,
    verify_password,
)
from database import DocumentORM, UserORM, get_db, init_db
from models import (
    DocumentCreate,
    LoginRequest,
    SearchRequest,
    SearchResponse,
    Token,
    TokenData,
)
from search import search_documents
import math

app = FastAPI(
    title="Document Search API",
    description="Authenticated, org-scoped document search",
    version="1.0.0",
)


@app.on_event("startup")
def on_startup():
    init_db()
    _seed_demo_data()


# ── Auth ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@app.post("/auth/login", response_model=Token, tags=["Auth"])
def login(
    form_data: Annotated[OAuth2PasswordRequestForm, Depends()],
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    user = db.query(UserORM).filter(UserORM.username == form_data.username).first()
    if not user or not verify_password(form_data.password, user.hashed_password):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Incorrect username or password",
            headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
        )
    token = create_access_token({"sub": user.user_id, "org_id": user.org_id})
    return Token(access_token=token, token_type="bearer")


# ── Documents ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@app.post("/documents", status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED, tags=["Documents"])
def create_document(
    payload: DocumentCreate,
    current_user: TokenData = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Add a document to the caller's organisation."""
    if payload.org_id != current_user.org_id:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail="Cannot create documents for a different organisation.",
        )
    doc = DocumentORM(**payload.model_dump())
    db.add(doc)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(doc)
    return {"id": doc.id, "message": "Document created."}


# ── Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@app.get("/documents/search", response_model=SearchResponse, tags=["Search"])
def search(
    keyword: str = Query(min_length=1, max_length=100),
    category: str | None = Query(default=None),
    page: int = Query(default=1, ge=1),
    page_size: int = Query(default=10, ge=1, le=50),
    current_user: TokenData = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """
    Search documents within the authenticated user's organisation.

    - **keyword**: required search term (matched against title + content)
    - **category**: optional filter (`legal`, `finance`, `hr`, `engineering`, `marketing`, `general`)
    - **page** / **page_size**: pagination controls
    """
    from models import DocumentCategory

    # Validate category string if provided
    cat = None
    if category:
        try:
            cat = DocumentCategory(category)
        except ValueError:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
                detail=f"Invalid category '{category}'. "
                       f"Choose from: {[e.value for e in DocumentCategory]}",
            )

    results, total = search_documents(
        db=db,
        org_id=current_user.org_id,
        keyword=keyword,
        category=cat,
        page=page,
        page_size=page_size,
    )

    return SearchResponse(
        keyword=keyword,
        category=cat,
        total_matches=total,
        page=page,
        page_size=page_size,
        total_pages=math.ceil(total / page_size) if total else 0,
        results=results,
    )


# ── Demo seed data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _seed_demo_data():
    """Populate the DB with two orgs and sample docs on first run."""
    db = next(get_db())

    if db.query(UserORM).count():
        return  # already seeded

    orgs = {
        "org-alpha": [
            ("alice", "password123"),
            ("bob",   "password456"),
        ],
        "org-beta": [
            ("carol", "password789"),
        ],
    }

    for org_id, members in orgs.items():
        for username, password in members:
            db.add(UserORM(
                username=username,
                hashed_password=hash_password(password),
                org_id=org_id,
                user_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            ))

    sample_docs = [
        ("Q1 Budget Report",      "finance",     "org-alpha",
         "Revenue increased by 15% in Q1. Budget allocation for marketing was exceeded."),
        ("Employee Handbook",     "hr",          "org-alpha",
         "All employees must complete onboarding within 30 days of joining."),
        ("API Design Guidelines", "engineering", "org-alpha",
         "Use RESTful conventions. All endpoints must require authentication tokens."),
        ("NDA Template",          "legal",       "org-alpha",
         "This Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into between the parties..."),
        ("Marketing Strategy",    "marketing",   "org-alpha",
         "Our Q2 marketing budget focuses on digital channels and influencer outreach."),
        ("Beta Financials",       "finance",     "org-beta",
         "Beta org confidential financial data. Revenue projections for next quarter."),
    ]

    for title, cat, org_id, content in sample_docs:
        db.add(DocumentORM(
            title=title,
            content=content,
            category=cat,
            org_id=org_id,
            owner_id="seed",
        ))

    db.commit()
    db.close()
```

### Example Usage

**1. Login**
```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8000/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "username=alice&password=password123"
# → {"access_token": "eyJ...", "token_type": "bearer"}
```

**2. Search all categories**
```bash
curl "http://localhost:8000/documents/search?keyword=budget" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJ..."
```

**3. Search with category filter + pagination**
```bash
curl "http://localhost:8000/documents/search?keyword=revenue&category=finance&page=1&page_size=5" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJ..."
```

**4. Example response**
```json
{
  "keyword": "budget",
  "category": null,
  "total_matches": 2,
  "page": 1,
  "page_size": 10,
  "total_pages": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Q1 Budget Report",
      "category": "finance",
      "owner_id": "seed",
      "snippet": "Revenue increased by 15% in Q1. Budget allocation for marketing was exceeded.",
      "relevance_score": 0.64
    },
    {
      "id": 5,
      "title": "Marketing Strategy",
      "category": "marketing",
      "owner_id": "seed",
      "snippet": "Our Q2 marketing budget focuses on digital channels and influencer outreach.",
      "relevance_score": 0.04
    }
  ]
}
```

---

### Key Security & Design Decisions

| Concern | Approach |
|---|---|
| **Org isolation** | Every query is hard-scoped to `current_user.org_id` — users cannot see other orgs' data |
| **Auth** | JWT Bearer tokens via `python-jose`; token carries `user_id` + `org_id` |
| **Password storage** | `bcrypt` via `passlib` — never stored in plain text |
| **Input validation** | Pydantic models + FastAPI `Query()` constraints on all inputs |
| **SQL injection** | SQLAlchemy ORM parameterised queries throughout |
| **Pagination** | Enforced `page_size` max of 50 to prevent large data dumps |

# Document Search API Endpoint

I'll build this systematically, then audit every checklist before shipping.

## Architecture decisions (pre-code)

- **Full-text search**: PostgreSQL `tsvector`/`tsquery` via parameterized query — no raw interpolation
- **Auth**: JWT bearer (validated `iss`/`aud`/`exp`/`alg`), tenant derived from token claims only
- **Tenant isolation**: RLS + mandatory `tenant_id` predicate in every query
- **Pagination**: keyset cursor (no offset)
- **Rate limiting**: per-principal sliding window in Redis
- **Logging**: structured wide events (structlog → JSON)

```python
"""
Centralised settings — all secrets from environment, never hardcoded.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from functools import lru_cache
from typing import Literal

from pydantic import AnyHttpUrl, Field, field_validator
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    # ── JWT ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    jwt_algorithms: list[str] = ["RS256"]          # allowlist; never "none" or HS*
    jwt_issuer: str
    jwt_audience: str
    jwt_public_key_pem: str                        # loaded from secret store / env

    # ── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    database_url: str                              # postgresql+asyncpg://...
    db_statement_timeout_ms: int = 5_000           # per-query hard ceiling

    # ── Redis (rate limiting) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    redis_url: str                                 # redis://...

    # ── Rate limiting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    rate_limit_requests: int = 60                  # per window
    rate_limit_window_seconds: int = 60
    rate_limit_burst: int = 10                     # extra burst allowance

    # ── Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    search_max_limit: int = 50
    search_default_limit: int = 20
    search_max_keyword_length: int = 200
    search_max_category_length: int = 100

    # ── Observability ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    log_level: Literal["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR"] = "INFO"
    environment: Literal["development", "staging", "production"] = "production"
    service_version: str = "unknown"

    # ── Transport ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # TLS termination happens at the load balancer/gateway (stated explicitly —
    # see BUILD non-negotiables §(b)).  The app itself binds only on loopback
    # (127.0.0.1) inside the container; no plaintext listener is reachable from
    # outside.  HSTS is emitted by the gateway (documented in ops runbook).
    # If running without a gateway (dev), set enforce_https=True to add HSTS here.
    enforce_https: bool = False

    @field_validator("jwt_algorithms")
    @classmethod
    def _no_none_alg(cls, v: list[str]) -> list[str]:
        forbidden = {"none", "hs256", "hs384", "hs512"}
        bad = forbidden.intersection({a.lower() for a in v})
        if bad:
            raise ValueError(f"Forbidden JWT algorithms: {bad}")
        return v

    model_config = {"env_file": ".env", "env_file_encoding": "utf-8"}


@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()  # type: ignore[call-arg]
```

## `app/logging_config.py`

```python
"""
Structured JSON logging via structlog.
Redaction filter runs at the processor level — not per call-site.
All PII/secrets are blocked centrally (rules/07 §2, logging-rules §4).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import sys
from typing import Any

import structlog

# ── Redaction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

_SECRET_KEYS = re.compile(
    r"(?i)(pass(word)?|token|secret|authorization|api[_\-]?key|cookie|ssn|cvv|card_?number)"
)
_JWT_SHAPE = re.compile(r"eyJ[\w\-]{10,}\.[\w\-]{10,}\.[\w\-]+")
_CRLF = re.compile(r"[\r\n]")


def _redact_processor(
    logger: Any, method: str, event_dict: dict[str, Any]
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """
    1. Redact values whose key matches a sensitive-key pattern.
    2. Strip JWT-shaped strings from all values.
    3. Escape CR/LF to prevent log-injection (CWE-117).
    """
    clean: dict[str, Any] = {}
    for k, v in event_dict.items():
        if _SECRET_KEYS.search(k):
            clean[k] = "[REDACTED]"
        elif isinstance(v, str):
            v = _JWT_SHAPE.sub("[JWT]", v)
            v = _CRLF.sub(" ", v)          # CWE-117: no log-forging via user input
            clean[k] = v
        else:
            clean[k] = v
    return clean


def configure_logging(log_level: str = "INFO") -> None:
    structlog.configure(
        processors=[
            structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,   # trace_id, user_id, tenant_id
            structlog.stdlib.add_log_level,
            structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name,
            structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso", utc=True),
            _redact_processor,
            structlog.processors.StackInfoRenderer(),
            structlog.processors.ExceptionRenderer(),  # single JSON field, not multiline
            structlog.processors.JSONRenderer(),
        ],
        wrapper_class=structlog.make_filtering_bound_logger(
            getattr(logging, log_level)
        ),
        context_class=dict,
        logger_factory=structlog.PrintLoggerFactory(file=sys.stdout),
        cache_logger_on_first_use=True,
    )
```

## `app/auth/jwt.py`

```python
"""
JWT validation.  Enforces iss/aud/exp/alg allowlist.  Never trusts
tenant_id from the request — always derived from validated token claims.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Annotated

import structlog
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, HTTPBearer

import jwt  # PyJWT ≥ 2.x

from app.config import Settings, get_settings

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

_bearer_scheme = HTTPBearer(auto_error=True)


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class AuthenticatedPrincipal:
    """
    Everything the app needs to know about the caller.
    Immutable — handlers cannot accidentally mutate claims.
    """
    user_id: str
    tenant_id: str
    email: str           # kept for audit logging only — never returned in search results
    scopes: frozenset[str]


def _decode_token(token: str, settings: Settings) -> dict:
    """
    Decode and validate a JWT.  Raises HTTPException on any failure.
    Algorithm allowlist prevents alg:none and HS/RS confusion attacks.
    """
    try:
        claims = jwt.decode(
            token,
            settings.jwt_public_key_pem,
            algorithms=settings.jwt_algorithms,   # allowlist, never ["*"]
            audience=settings.jwt_audience,
            issuer=settings.jwt_issuer,
            options={
                "require": ["exp", "iat", "sub", "iss", "aud"],
                "verify_exp": True,
                "verify_iat": True,
            },
        )
    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="token_expired")
    except jwt.InvalidAudienceError:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="invalid_audience")
    except jwt.InvalidIssuerError:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="invalid_issuer")
    except jwt.InvalidAlgorithmError:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="invalid_algorithm")
    except jwt.PyJWTError:
        # Generic — do not expose which check failed (oracle prevention)
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="invalid_token")
    return claims


async def get_current_principal(
    credentials: Annotated[HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, Depends(_bearer_scheme)],
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> AuthenticatedPrincipal:
    claims = _decode_token(credentials.credentials, settings)

    # tenant_id MUST come from the validated token — never from the request body
    # or query string (rules/03 §5, API-security §7).
    tenant_id = claims.get("tenant_id") or claims.get("org_id")
    if not tenant_id:
        log.warning("jwt_missing_tenant", sub=claims.get("sub"))
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="missing_tenant_claim")

    return AuthenticatedPrincipal(
        user_id=claims["sub"],
        tenant_id=tenant_id,
        email=claims.get("email", ""),
        scopes=frozenset(claims.get("scp", "").split()),
    )


# Convenience alias used in route signatures
CurrentPrincipal = Annotated[AuthenticatedPrincipal, Depends(get_current_principal)]
```

## `app/db/connection.py`

```python
"""
Async SQLAlchemy engine + per-request session factory.
statement_timeout is set at the session level so no single query can
hold a connection forever (rules/07 §4, API-security §4).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from typing import Annotated

import structlog
from fastapi import Depends
from sqlalchemy import event, text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, async_sessionmaker, create_async_engine

from app.config import Settings, get_settings

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

_engines: dict[str, object] = {}


def _get_engine(settings: Settings):
    url = settings.database_url
    if url not in _engines:
        _engines[url] = create_async_engine(
            url,
            pool_size=10,
            max_overflow=5,
            pool_pre_ping=True,
            echo=False,             # never echo SQL in prod (leaks schema)
        )
    return _engines[url]


@asynccontextmanager
async def _session_ctx(settings: Settings) -> AsyncGenerator[AsyncSession, None]:
    engine = _get_engine(settings)
    factory = async_sessionmaker(engine, expire_on_commit=False, class_=AsyncSession)
    async with factory() as session:
        # Per-session statement timeout — defence against runaway queries.
        # This also protects against slow injection payloads.
        await session.execute(
            text(f"SET LOCAL statement_timeout = '{settings.db_statement_timeout_ms}ms'")
        )
        yield session


async def get_db_session(
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> AsyncGenerator[AsyncSession, None]:
    async with _session_ctx(settings) as session:
        yield session


DBSession = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db_session)]
```

## `app/middleware/correlation.py`

```python
"""
Injects a W3C Trace Context trace_id into every request.
Binds it to structlog's contextvars so every log line in the request
carries it automatically — no per-call-site wiring needed.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import uuid

import structlog
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.responses import Response


class CorrelationMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next) -> Response:
        # Accept inbound traceparent (W3C Trace Context) or generate one.
        traceparent = request.headers.get("traceparent")
        if traceparent:
            # Extract trace-id portion (field 2 of "version-traceid-parentid-flags")
            parts = traceparent.split("-")
            trace_id = parts[1] if len(parts) >= 2 else str(uuid.uuid4().hex)
        else:
            trace_id = uuid.uuid4().hex

        # Bind to structlog contextvars — automatically attached to every log line
        structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars()
        structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(trace_id=trace_id)

        response = await call_next(request)
        response.headers["X-Trace-Id"] = trace_id   # returned on every response
        return response
```

## `app/middleware/rate_limit.py`

```python
"""
Sliding-window rate limiter backed by Redis (atomic Lua script).

Keyed on authenticated principal (user_id) for authed routes.
Falls back to IP for unauthenticated surfaces.

Returns standard IETF RateLimit headers on every response (success and 429).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
from typing import Annotated

import structlog
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, Request, status
from redis.asyncio import Redis

from app.config import Settings, get_settings

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

# ── Lua script: atomic sliding-window counter ────────────────────────────────
# Returns [current_count, ttl_ms_remaining]
_SLIDING_WINDOW_LUA = """
local key    = KEYS[1]
local now    = tonumber(ARGV[1])
local window = tonumber(ARGV[2])
local limit  = tonumber(ARGV[3])
local burst  = tonumber(ARGV[4])

local window_start = now - window * 1000

-- Remove timestamps outside the window
redis.call('ZREMRANGEBYSCORE', key, '-inf', window_start)

local count = redis.call('ZCARD', key)
local effective_limit = limit + burst

if count >= effective_limit then
    local oldest = redis.call('ZRANGE', key, 0, 0, 'WITHSCORES')
    local retry_after = 0
    if #oldest > 0 then
        retry_after = math.ceil((tonumber(oldest[2]) + window * 1000 - now) / 1000)
    end
    return {count, retry_after, 0}   -- 0 = denied
end

-- Add this request
redis.call('ZADD', key, now, now .. '-' .. math.random(1e9))
redis.call('PEXPIRE', key, window * 1000)

local remaining = effective_limit - count - 1
local reset_in  = window   -- approximate
return {count + 1, reset_in, 1}   -- 1 = allowed
"""


_redis_client: Redis | None = None


def _get_redis(settings: Settings) -> Redis:
    global _redis_client
    if _redis_client is None:
        _redis_client = Redis.from_url(
            settings.redis_url,
            decode_responses=False,
            socket_timeout=0.5,       # fast fail — don't block requests
            socket_connect_timeout=0.5,
        )
    return _redis_client


async def check_rate_limit(
    request: Request,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> None:
    """
    FastAPI dependency.  Raises 429 if the principal is over limit.
    Keyed on user_id (from JWT, set by auth middleware earlier in the chain)
    or falls back to IP for unauthenticated endpoints.
    """
    # The auth middleware runs before this; principal is in request.state
    principal = getattr(request.state, "principal", None)
    key_id = principal.user_id if principal else _client_ip(request)
    redis_key = f"rl:search:{key_id}"

    redis = _get_redis(settings)
    now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)

    try:
        result = await redis.eval(
            _SLIDING_WINDOW_LUA,
            1,
            redis_key,
            now_ms,
            settings.rate_limit_window_seconds,
            settings.rate_limit_requests,
            settings.rate_limit_burst,
        )
        count, retry_or_reset, allowed = int(result[0]), int(result[1]), int(result[2])
    except Exception as exc:
        # Redis unavailable — fail OPEN here with a warning, because rate
        # limiting is handled at the gateway layer as primary defence.
        # If this service is the sole enforcement point, change to fail closed.
        log.warning(
            "rate_limit_redis_error",
            error=str(exc),
            key_id=key_id,
        )
        return

    limit = settings.rate_limit_requests + settings.rate_limit_burst
    remaining = max(0, limit - count)

    # Attach headers to the *request* so the response middleware can copy them
    request.state.rate_limit_headers = {
        "RateLimit-Policy": f'"search";q={limit};w={settings.rate_limit_window_seconds}',
        "RateLimit": f'"search";r={remaining};t={retry_or_reset}',
        "X-RateLimit-Limit": str(limit),
        "X-RateLimit-Remaining": str(remaining),
        "X-RateLimit-Reset": str(retry_or_reset),
    }

    if not allowed:
        log.warning(
            "rate_limit_exceeded",
            key_id=key_id,
            count=count,
            retry_after=retry_or_reset,
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail={
                "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/rate-limited",
                "title": "Rate limit exceeded",
                "status": 429,
                "detail": (
                    f"Limit {settings.rate_limit_requests} requests/"
                    f"{settings.rate_limit_window_seconds}s exceeded."
                ),
                "retry_after": retry_or_reset,
            },
            headers={"Retry-After": str(retry_or_reset)},
        )


def _client_ip(request: Request) -> str:
    # X-Forwarded-For is only trusted if the gateway strips/sets it.
    # Adjust to your infrastructure.
    forwarded = request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For")
    if forwarded:
        return forwarded.split(",")[0].strip()
    return request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
```

## `app/middleware/logging_middleware.py`

```python
"""
Wide-event middleware: builds one rich JSON log line per request (logging-rules §5).
Fires on both success and exception paths.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
from typing import Any

import structlog
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.responses import Response

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


class WideEventMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next) -> Response:
        start = time.perf_counter()
        evt: dict[str, Any] = {
            "http.request.method": request.method,
            "http.route": request.url.path,
            "http.query": _scrub_query(str(request.url.query)),
        }

        exc_info = None
        response: Response | None = None
        try:
            response = await call_next(request)
            evt["http.response.status_code"] = response.status_code
            evt["outcome"] = "success" if response.status_code < 400 else "error"
        except Exception as exc:
            evt["http.response.status_code"] = 500
            evt["outcome"] = "error"
            evt["error.type"] = type(exc).__name__
            exc_info = exc
            raise
        finally:
            evt["duration_ms"] = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)

            # Attach principal context if auth middleware set it
            principal = getattr(request.state, "principal", None)
            if principal:
                evt["user_id"] = principal.user_id
                evt["tenant_id"] = principal.tenant_id
                # email intentionally omitted — PII, not needed in wide event

            # Copy rate-limit headers from request.state if set
            rl_headers = getattr(request.state, "rate_limit_headers", {})
            if response and rl_headers:
                for k, v in rl_headers.items():
                    response.headers[k] = v

            log.info("http_request", **evt)

        return response


def _scrub_query(query: str) -> str:
    """Remove token/key values from query strings before logging (CWE-598)."""
    import re
    return re.sub(
        r"(?i)(token|key|secret|password|api_key)=[^&]*",
        r"\1=[REDACTED]",
        query,
    )
```

## `app/schemas/documents.py`

```python
"""
Input and output schemas.  Explicit allowlists — no ORM entity serialization.
extra='forbid' on input DTOs prevents mass-assignment (rules/07 §3).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import base64
import json
from enum import Enum
from typing import Annotated
from uuid import UUID

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator


# ── Allowlisted categories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class DocumentCategory(str, Enum):
    CONTRACT = "contract"
    INVOICE = "invoice"
    REPORT = "report"
    POLICY = "policy"
    OTHER = "other"


# ── Request ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class DocumentSearchRequest(BaseModel):
    """
    Input DTO.  extra='forbid' rejects unknown fields.
    All fields length-limited and allowlist-validated.
    """
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    keyword: Annotated[
        str,
        Field(
            min_length=1,
            max_length=200,
            description="Full-text search keyword(s)",
        ),
    ]
    category: DocumentCategory | None = Field(
        default=None,
        description="Optional category filter",
    )
    limit: Annotated[
        int,
        Field(ge=1, le=50, default=20),
    ]
    cursor: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Field(default=None, max_length=512, description="Opaque pagination cursor"),
    ]

    @field_validator("keyword")
    @classmethod
    def _no_control_chars(cls, v: str) -> str:
        """Reject CR/LF and NUL to prevent log injection and SQL edge cases."""
        if any(c in v for c in ("\r", "\n", "\x00")):
            raise ValueError("keyword contains disallowed control characters")
        return v.strip()


# ── Cursor helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class SearchCursor(BaseModel):
    """Internal cursor structure — never exposed raw to clients."""
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    last_rank: float
    last_id: UUID

    def encode(self) -> str:
        payload = json.dumps({"r": self.last_rank, "id": str(self.last_id)})
        return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload.encode()).decode()

    @classmethod
    def decode(cls, token: str) -> "SearchCursor":
        try:
            payload = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(token.encode()))
            return cls(last_rank=float(payload["r"]), last_id=UUID(payload["id"]))
        except Exception:
            from fastapi import HTTPException, status
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                detail="invalid_cursor",
            )


# ── Output ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class DocumentResult(BaseModel):
    """
    Explicit output DTO — only fields safe to expose.
    Adding a DB column never silently changes the API contract.
    """
    id: UUID
    title: str
    category: DocumentCategory
    snippet: str          # headline excerpt from FTS, HTML-stripped
    score: float
    created_at: str       # RFC 3339 UTC


class DocumentSearchResponse(BaseModel):
    data: list[DocumentResult]
    next_cursor: str | None
    has_more: bool
    # total deliberately omitted — COUNT(*) on large tables is a self-DoS.
    # Clients can infer "more pages exist" from has_more.
```

## `app/services/document_search.py`

```python
"""
Document search service.

Security properties:
- All SQL is fully parameterized (rules/01 §2).
- tenant_id is injected from the validated JWT claim, never from the request.
- Every query is scoped to the caller's tenant (rules/03 §5).
- FTS uses plainto_tsquery (safe) not to_tsquery (injectable with raw operators).
- Cursor values are validated and typed before use — never interpolated.
- ORDER BY columns are hardcoded, not user-controlled.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from uuid import UUID

import structlog
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from app.schemas.documents import (
    DocumentCategory,
    DocumentResult,
    DocumentSearchResponse,
    SearchCursor,
)

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

# Allowlist for sort columns — user cannot inject arbitrary column names.
# Currently only one sort (relevance + id), but the pattern is here for extension.
_ALLOWED_SORT_COLS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"rank", "created_at"})


async def search_documents(
    *,
    session: AsyncSession,
    tenant_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    keyword: str,
    category: DocumentCategory | None,
    limit: int,
    cursor: SearchCursor | None,
) -> DocumentSearchResponse:
    """
    Returns paginated full-text search results scoped to the caller's tenant.

    Uses keyset pagination on (rank DESC, id DESC) for O(1) per page.
    plainto_tsquery normalises the keyword safely — no operator injection.
    """

    # ── Build parameterized query ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    # All values are bound parameters.  ORDER BY and column names are
    # hardcoded — never interpolated from user input (rules/01 §2).
    #
    # The query deliberately includes tenant_id in the WHERE clause even though
    # Postgres RLS enforces it at the DB layer — defence in depth (rules/03 §5).

    params: dict = {
        "tenant_id": tenant_id,
        "query": keyword,           # plainto_tsquery handles sanitisation
        "lim": limit + 1,           # fetch one extra to determine has_more
    }

    category_filter = ""
    if category is not None:
        category_filter = "AND d.category = :category"
        params["category"] = category.value

    cursor_filter = ""
    if cursor is not None:
        # Keyset: continue after the last seen (rank, id) pair.
        # Both values come from a validated SearchCursor — never raw user input.
        cursor_filter = """
            AND (
                ts_rank(d.search_vector, plainto_tsquery('english', :query)) < :last_rank
                OR (
                    ts_rank(d.search_vector, plainto_tsquery('english', :query)) = :last_rank
                    AND d.id < :last_id
                )
            )
        """
        params["last_rank"] = cursor.last_rank
        params["last_id"] = str(cursor.last_id)

    # plainto_tsquery is safe: it treats the entire input as plain text,
    # never interpreting FTS operators like & | ! <-> that to_tsquery would.
    sql = text(f"""
        SELECT
            d.id,
            d.title,
            d.category,
            ts_headline(
                'english',
                d.content,
                plainto_tsquery('english', :query),
                'MaxWords=20, MinWords=5, ShortWord=3, HighlightAll=false'
            ) AS snippet,
            ts_rank(d.search_vector, plainto_tsquery('english', :query)) AS rank,
            to_char(d.created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS"Z"') AS created_at
        FROM documents d
        WHERE
            d.tenant_id  = :tenant_id
            AND d.deleted_at IS NULL
            AND d.search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', :query)
            {category_filter}
            {cursor_filter}
        ORDER BY rank DESC, d.id DESC
        LIMIT :lim
    """)
    # NOTE: category_filter and cursor_filter are STATIC strings with only
    # :named_params — they contain no user data.  The f-string interpolation
    # here is safe because these fragments are defined above in this function
    # body, not derived from any external input.

    rows = (await session.execute(sql, params)).mappings().all()

    # ── Determine pagination ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    has_more = len(rows) > limit
    page_rows = rows[:limit]

    next_cursor: str | None = None
    if has_more and page_rows:
        last = page_rows[-1]
        next_cursor = SearchCursor(
            last_rank=float(last["rank"]),
            last_id=UUID(str(last["id"])),
        ).encode()

    # ── Map to output DTOs — explicit allowlist, no ORM entity dump ───────────
    results = [
        DocumentResult(
            id=row["id"],
            title=row["title"],
            category=DocumentCategory(row["category"]),
            snippet=_strip_html(row["snippet"] or ""),
            score=round(float(row["rank"]), 4),
            created_at=row["created_at"],
        )
        for row in page_rows
    ]

    log.info(
        "document_search_executed",
        tenant_id=tenant_id,
        user_id=user_id,
        result_count=len(results),
        has_more=has_more,
        has_cursor=cursor is not None,
        has_category_filter=category is not None,
        # keyword intentionally NOT logged — may contain PII search terms
    )

    return DocumentSearchResponse(
        data=results,
        next_cursor=next_cursor,
        has_more=has_more,
    )


def _strip_html(text: str) -> str:
    """
    Remove ts_headline HTML tags (<b>) from snippets before returning to clients.
    Never return raw HTML in a JSON API — XSS risk if clients render it.
    """
    import re
    return re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", text)
```

## `app/routers/documents.py`

```python
"""
Document search router.

Dependency chain (in order of execution):
  CorrelationMiddleware → WideEventMiddleware → get_current_principal
  → check_rate_limit → search endpoint

Authorization: deny-by-default; this endpoint requires 'documents:read' scope.
Tenant isolation: tenant_id from JWT claim only — never from request params.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import structlog
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Request, status

from app.auth.jwt import CurrentPrincipal
from app.db.connection import DBSession
from app.middleware.rate_limit import check_rate_limit
from app.schemas.documents import (
    DocumentSearchRequest,
    DocumentSearchResponse,
    SearchCursor,
)
from app.schemas.errors import ProblemDetail
from app.services.document_search import search_documents

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/v1/documents", tags=["documents"])

_REQUIRED_SCOPE = "documents:read"


@router.post(
    "/search",
    response_model=DocumentSearchResponse,
    status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK,
    responses={
        400: {"model": ProblemDetail, "description": "Invalid request"},
        401: {"model": ProblemDetail, "description": "Not authenticated"},
        403: {"model": ProblemDetail, "description": "Insufficient scope"},
        429: {"model": ProblemDetail, "description": "Rate limited"},
        500: {"model": ProblemDetail, "description": "Internal error"},
    },
    summary="Search documents",
    description=(
        "Full-text search over the authenticated user's organisation documents. "
        "Requires scope `documents:read`. Results are scoped to the caller's tenant. "
        "Supports cursor-based pagination."
    ),
    # POST because the search body may exceed URL length limits.
    # Idempotent by nature (read-only), so no idempotency key needed.
    # Method: POST /searches would also be valid REST; /search is kept for
    # discoverability in this single-endpoint design.
)
async def search_documents_endpoint(
    request: Request,
    body: DocumentSearchRequest,
    principal: CurrentPrincipal,
    session: DBSession,
    _rate_limit: None = Depends(check_rate_limit),
) -> DocumentSearchResponse:
    """
    Search documents.

    Authorization: requires 'documents:read' scope in the JWT.
    Tenant isolation: results are always scoped to the JWT's tenant_id.
    """
    # ── Function-level authorization (rules/03 §3) ────────────────────────────
    if _REQUIRED_SCOPE not in principal.scopes:
        log.warning(
            "authz_denied",
            user_id=principal.user_id,
            tenant_id=principal.tenant_id,
            required_scope=_REQUIRED_SCOPE,
            action="document_search",
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail={
                "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/forbidden",
                "title": "Insufficient scope",
                "status": 403,
                "detail": f"Scope '{_REQUIRED_SCOPE}' required.",
            },
        )

    # Bind principal to structlog context — appears in all subsequent log lines
    # for this request without repeating at every call site.
    structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(
        user_id=principal.user_id,
        tenant_id=principal.tenant_id,
    )
    # Also store on request.state so WideEventMiddleware can attach it
    request.state.principal = principal

    # ── Decode cursor (validated; raises 400 on tamper) ───────────────────────
    cursor: SearchCursor | None = None
    if body.cursor:
        cursor = SearchCursor.decode(body.cursor)

    # ── Execute search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    return await search_documents(
        session=session,
        tenant_id=principal.tenant_id,     # from JWT — never from request body
        user_id=principal.user_id,
        keyword=body.keyword,
        category=body.category,
        limit=body.limit,
        cursor=cursor,
    )
```

## `app/main.py`

```python
"""
FastAPI application factory.
Global exception handler converts all unhandled errors to generic RFC 9457
responses — stack traces never reach clients (rules/07 §1).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, status
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

from app.config import get_settings
from app.logging_config import configure_logging
from app.middleware.correlation import CorrelationMiddleware
from app.middleware.logging_middleware import WideEventMiddleware
from app.routers import documents

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    settings = get_settings()
    configure_logging(settings.log_level)
    log.info("app_startup", environment=settings.environment, version=settings.service_version)
    yield
    log.info("app_shutdown")


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()

    app = FastAPI(
        title="Document Search API",
        version="1.0.0",
        # Disable automatic /docs and /redoc in production (CWE-489 / rules/07 §6)
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.environment != "production" else None,
        redoc_url="/redoc" if settings.environment != "production" else None,
        openapi_url="/openapi.json" if settings.environment != "production" else None,
        lifespan=lifespan,
    )

    # ── Middleware (outermost first) ──────────────────────────────────────────
    # Order: CorrelationMiddleware wraps WideEventMiddleware so trace_id is
    # available when the wide event fires.
    app.add_middleware(WideEventMiddleware)
    app.add_middleware(CorrelationMiddleware)

    # ── HSTS (when not handled at gateway) ───────────────────────────────────
    if settings.enforce_https:
        from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import HTTPSRedirectMiddleware
        app.add_middleware(HTTPSRedirectMiddleware)

    # ── Routers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    app.include_router(documents.router)

    # ── Exception handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    @app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
    async def validation_error_handler(request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError):
        """Pydantic validation failures → RFC 9457 422 with all errors batched."""
        trace_id = getattr(
            structlog.contextvars.get_contextvars(), "trace_id", str(uuid.uuid4().hex)
        )
        errors = [
            {
                "pointer": "/" + "/".join(str(loc) for loc in e["loc"]),
                "code": e["type"],
                "message": e["msg"],
            }
            for e in exc.errors()
        ]
        log.warning(
            "request_validation_failed",
            error_count=len(errors),
            path=request.url.path,
        )
        return JSONResponse(
            status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
            content={
                "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/validation",
                "title": "Validation failed",
                "status": 422,
                "detail": f"{len(errors)} field(s) failed validation.",
                "errors": errors,
                "trace_id": trace_id,
            },
        )

    @app.exception_handler(Exception)
    async def global_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception):
        """
        Catch-all: full detail goes to logs, generic message to client.
        Stack traces NEVER reach the client (rules/07 §1).
        """
        ctx = structlog.contextvars.get_contextvars()
        trace_id = ctx.get("trace_id", uuid.uuid4().hex)
        log.exception(
            "unhandled_exception",
            error_type=type(exc).__name__,
            path=request.url.path,
            trace_id=trace_id,
        )
        return JSONResponse(
            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
            content={
                "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/internal",
                "title": "Internal server error",
                "status": 500,
                "detail": "An unexpected error occurred.",
                "trace_id": trace_id,
            },
        )

    return app


app = create_app()
```

## `app/db/migrations/001_documents_fts.sql`

```sql
-- Migration: documents table with full-text search and RLS
-- Apply with your migration tool (Alembic, Flyway, etc.)

BEGIN;

-- ── Table ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
    id           UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id    UUID        NOT NULL,
    owner_id     UUID        NOT NULL,
    title        TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(title)   BETWEEN 1 AND 500),
    content      TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(content) <= 1_000_000),
    category     TEXT        NOT NULL
                             CHECK (category IN ('contract','invoice','report','policy','other')),
    search_vector TSVECTOR   GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
                                 to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title,'') || ' ' || coalesce(content,''))
                             ) STORED,
    created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    deleted_at   TIMESTAMPTZ          -- soft-delete; all queries must filter IS NULL
);

-- ── Indexes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- GIN for full-text search
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_fts
    ON documents USING GIN (search_vector);

-- Composite for keyset pagination: equality (tenant_id) + sort columns (rank is
-- computed; we index on created_at as a proxy for non-FTS ordering)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_tenant_created
    ON documents (tenant_id, created_at DESC)
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Partial index for category filter (avoids full-table scans on common queries)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_tenant_category
    ON documents (tenant_id, category, created_at DESC)
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

-- ── Row-Level Security ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- RLS is the structural enforcement layer.  The application ALSO filters on
-- tenant_id in every query (defence in depth).
ALTER TABLE documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE documents FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;   -- applies even to table owner

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON documents
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::uuid);

-- The application role must NOT have BYPASSRLS or be a superuser.
-- Grant only what's needed:
-- GRANT SELECT ON documents TO app_role;

COMMENT ON TABLE documents IS
    'Tenant-isolated document store. RLS enforces tenant_id from app.tenant_id session variable.';

COMMIT;
```

```python
"""
Tests for the document search service and endpoint.
Covers: happy path, pagination, category filter, empty results,
        invalid inputs, cursor tamper, SQL injection attempt.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import uuid
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio
from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.auth.jwt import AuthenticatedPrincipal
from app.schemas.documents import DocumentSearchRequest, SearchCursor
from app.services.document_search import search_documents
from app.tests.conftest import make_doc_row


# ── Service unit tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestSearchDocumentsService:
    """Unit tests: mock the DB session, test service logic in isolation."""

    def _make_session(self, rows):
        session = AsyncMock()
        mapping = MagicMock()
        mapping.all.return_value = rows
        execute_result = MagicMock()
        execute_result.mappings.return_value = mapping
        session.execute = AsyncMock(return_value=execute_result)
        return session

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_returns_results(self, principal_a):
        tid = principal_a.tenant_id
        rows = [make_doc_row(tid) for _ in range(3)]
        session = self._make_session(rows)

        result = await search_documents(
            session=session,
            tenant_id=tid,
            user_id=principal_a.user_id,
            keyword="quarterly report",
            category=None,
            limit=20,
            cursor=None,
        )

        assert len(result.data) == 3
        assert result.has_more is False
        assert result.next_cursor is None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_pagination_has_more(self, principal_a):
        tid = principal_a.tenant_id
        # Service fetches limit+1; if len > limit → has_more
        rows = [make_doc_row(tid) for _ in range(21)]  # limit=20, fetched 21
        session = self._make_session(rows)

        result = await search_documents(
            session=session,
            tenant_id=tid,
            user_id=principal_a.user_id,
            keyword="budget",
            category=None,
            limit=20,
            cursor=None,
        )

        assert len(result.data) == 20
        assert result.has_more is True
        assert result.next_cursor is not None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_cursor_encodes_and_decodes(self, principal_a):
        tid = principal_a.tenant_id
        rows = [make_doc_row(tid) for _ in range(21)]
        session = self._make_session(rows)

        result = await search_documents(
            session=session,
            tenant_id=tid,
            user_id=principal_a.user_id,
            keyword="policy",
            category=None,
            limit=20,
            cursor=None,
        )

        cursor = SearchCursor.decode(result.next_cursor)
        assert cursor.last_rank >= 0
        assert cursor.last_id is not None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_empty_results(self, principal_a):
        session = self._make_session([])

        result = await search_documents(
            session=session,
            tenant_id=principal_a.tenant_id,
            user_id=principal_a.user_id,
            keyword="zzznomatch",
            category=None,
            limit=20,
            cursor=None,
        )

        assert result.data == []
        assert result.has_more is False
        assert result.next_cursor is None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_snippet_html_stripped(self, principal_a):
        tid = principal_a.tenant_id
        rows = [make_doc_row(tid, snippet="This is <b>important</b> text.")]
        session = self._make_session(rows)

        result = await search_documents(
            session=session,
            tenant_id=tid,
            user_id=principal_a.user_id,
            keyword="important",
            category=None,
            limit=20,
            cursor=None,
        )

        assert "<b>" not in result.data[0].snippet
        assert "important" in result.data[0].snippet

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_tenant_id_is_always_from_principal(self, principal_a):
        """Verify tenant_id passed to the query comes from the principal, not from
        any request field — structural test of the service signature."""
        session = self._make_session([])
        await search_documents(
            session=session,
            tenant_id=principal_a.tenant_id,
            user_id=principal_a.user_id,
            keyword="test",
            category=None,
            limit=5,
            cursor=None,
        )
        # Verify the SQL was executed with the correct tenant_id binding
        call_args = session.execute.call_args
        bound_params = call_args[0][1]  # second positional arg to execute()
        assert bound_params["tenant_id"] == principal_a.tenant_id


# ── Input validation tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestDocumentSearchRequest:
    def test_rejects_empty_keyword(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="", limit=10)

    def test_rejects_keyword_too_long(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="x" * 201, limit=10)

    def test_rejects_control_chars_in_keyword(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="hello\nworld", limit=10)

    def test_rejects_null_byte(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="hello\x00world", limit=10)

    def test_rejects_limit_over_max(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="test", limit=51)

    def test_rejects_unknown_fields(self):
        """extra='forbid' prevents mass-assignment."""
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="test", limit=10, role="admin")

    def test_rejects_invalid_category(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="test", limit=10, category="malicious")

    def test_valid_request(self):
        req = DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="quarterly report", limit=10, category="report")
        assert req.keyword == "quarterly report"

    def test_cursor_length_limit(self):
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            DocumentSearchRequest(keyword="test", limit=10, cursor="x" * 513)


class TestSearchCursor:
    def test_tampered_cursor_raises_400(self):
        from fastapi import HTTPException
        with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc_info:
            SearchCursor.decode("not-valid-base64!!!")
        assert exc_info.value.status_code == 400

    def test_roundtrip(self):
        original = SearchCursor(last_rank=0.75, last_id=uuid.uuid4())
        decoded = SearchCursor.decode(original.encode())
        assert decoded.last_rank == original.last_rank
        assert decoded.last_id == original.last_id


# ── HTTP endpoint integration tests ──────────────────────────────────────────

class TestSearchEndpoint:
    """Integration tests using the full ASGI stack with mocked DB and Redis."""

    def _mock_search(self, mocker, rows=None, principal=None):
        """Patch the search service and auth dependency."""
        from app.schemas.documents import DocumentSearchResponse
        mock_response = DocumentSearchResponse(data=[], next_cursor=None, has_more=False)

        mocker.patch(
            "app.routers.documents.search_documents",
            return_value=mock_response,
        )
        if principal:
            mocker.patch(
                "app.auth.jwt.get_current_principal",
                return_value=principal,
            )
        mocker.patch(
            "app.middleware.rate_limit.check_rate_limit",
            return_value=None,
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_returns_200_on_valid_request(self, async_client, principal_a, mocker):
        self._mock_search(mocker, principal=principal_a)
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "budget", "limit": 10},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        body = resp.json()
        assert "data" in body
        assert "has_more" in body

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_returns_422_on_empty_keyword(self, async_client, principal_a, mocker):
        self._mock_search(mocker, principal=principal_a)
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "", "limit": 10},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 422
        body = resp.json()
        assert body["type"] == "https://api.example.com/errors/validation"
        assert "errors" in body

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_returns_403_without_scope(self, async_client, mocker):
        """Principal with no documents:read scope → 403."""
        no_scope_principal = AuthenticatedPrincipal(
            user_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            tenant_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            email="x@y.com",
            scopes=frozenset(),   # no scope
        )
        self._mock_search(mocker, principal=no_scope_principal)
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "test", "limit": 10},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_returns_trace_id_header(self, async_client, principal_a, mocker):
        self._mock_search(mocker, principal=principal_a)
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "test", "limit": 5},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        assert "X-Trace-Id" in resp.headers

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_unknown_fields_rejected(self, async_client, principal_a, mocker):
        """extra='forbid' on the DTO means unknown fields return 422."""
        self._mock_search(mocker, principal=principal_a)
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "test", "limit": 5, "role": "admin"},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sql_injection_attempt_rejected_by_validation(
        self, async_client, principal_a, mocker
    ):
        """
        SQL injection in keyword: the validator strips control chars, and
        even if it passed, plainto_tsquery treats it as plain text.
        Verify the endpoint returns a safe response (not 500).
        """
        self._mock_search(mocker, principal=principal_a)
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "'; DROP TABLE documents; --", "limit": 5},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        # The keyword itself is valid text (no control chars) — passes validation,
        # but plainto_tsquery neutralises all SQL/FTS operators.
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_500_returns_generic_body_no_stacktrace(
        self, async_client, principal_a, mocker
    ):
        """Global exception handler: no stack traces in 500 responses."""
        mocker.patch(
            "app.auth.jwt.get_current_principal",
            return_value=principal_a,
        )
        mocker.patch(
            "app.middleware.rate_limit.check_rate_limit",
            return_value=None,
        )
        mocker.patch(
            "app.routers.documents.search_documents",
            side_effect=RuntimeError("DB exploded"),
        )
        resp = await async_client.post(
            "/api/v1/documents/search",
            json={"keyword": "test", "limit": 5},
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer fake.jwt.token"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 500
        body = resp.json()
        assert "trace_id" in body
        assert "DB exploded" not in str(body)       # no internal detail
        assert "RuntimeError" not in str(body)
        assert "traceback" not in str(body).lower()
```

```python
"""JWT validation tests."""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
import uuid
from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest
import jwt as pyjwt
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import rsa

from app.auth.jwt import _decode_token
from app.config import Settings


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def rsa_keypair():
    private_key = rsa.generate_private_key(public_exponent=65537, key_size=2048)
    public_pem = private_key.public_key().public_bytes(
        serialization.Encoding.PEM,
        serialization.PublicFormat.SubjectPublicKeyInfo,
    ).decode()
    return private_key, public_pem


@pytest.fixture
def settings_with_key(rsa_keypair) -> Settings:
    _, public_pem = rsa_keypair
    return Settings(
        jwt_algorithms=["RS256"],
        jwt_issuer="https://auth.example.com",
        jwt_audience="api.example.com",
        jwt_public_key_pem=public_pem,
        database_url="postgresql+asyncpg://x:x@localhost/x",
        redis_url="redis://localhost",
    )


def _make_token(private_key, overrides=None):
    payload = {
        "sub": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "iss": "https://auth.example.com",
        "aud": "api.example.com",
        "exp": int(time.time()) + 300,
        "iat": int(time.time()),
        "tenant_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "scp": "documents:read",
    }
    if overrides:
        payload.update(overrides)
    return pyjwt.encode(payload, private_key, algorithm="RS256")


class TestJWTValidation:
    def test_valid_token_decodes(self, rsa_keypair, settings_with_key):
        private_key, _ = rsa_keypair
        token = _make_token(private_key)
        claims = _decode_token(token, settings_with_key)
        assert "sub" in claims

    def test_expired_token_raises_401(self, rsa_keypair, settings_with_key):
        from fastapi import HTTPException
        private_key, _ = rsa_keypair
        token = _make_token(private_key, {"exp": int(time.time()) - 10})
        with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
            _decode_token(token, settings_with_key)
        assert exc.value.status_code == 401
        assert exc.value.detail == "token_expired"

    def test_wrong_audience_raises_401(self, rsa_keypair, settings_with_key):
        from fastapi import HTTPException
        private_key, _ = rsa_keypair
        token = _make_token(private_key, {"aud": "wrong-audience"})
        with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
            _decode_token(token, settings_with_key)
        assert exc.value.status_code == 401

    def test_wrong_issuer_raises_401(self, rsa_keypair, settings_with_key):
        from fastapi import HTTPException
        private_key, _ = rsa_keypair
        token = _make_token(private_key, {"iss": "https://evil.com"})
        with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
            _decode_token(token, settings_with_key)
        assert exc.value.status_code == 401

    def test_none_algorithm_rejected_by_config(self, settings_with_key):
        """alg:none must be in the algorithm denylist at config level."""
        assert "none" not in [a.lower() for a in settings_with_key.jwt_algorithms]

    def test_hs256_rejected_by_config(self, settings_with_key):
        assert "hs256" not in [a.lower() for a in settings_with_key.jwt_algorithms]
```

```python
"""Rate limiter unit tests — Redis interactions mocked."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch, MagicMock
from fastapi import HTTPException
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.datastructures import Headers

from app.middleware.rate_limit import check_rate_limit
from app.config import Settings


def _make_request(user_id: str | None = None) -> Request:
    scope = {
        "type": "http",
        "method": "POST",
        "path": "/api/v1/documents/search",
        "headers": [],
        "query_string": b"",
        "client": ("127.0.0.1", 12345),
    }
    req = Request(scope)
    if user_id:
        from app.auth.jwt import AuthenticatedPrincipal
        req.state.principal = AuthenticatedPrincipal(
            user_id=user_id,
            tenant_id="t1",
            email="x@y.com",
            scopes=frozenset({"documents:read"}),
        )
    return req


@pytest.fixture
def settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings(
        jwt_algorithms=["RS256"],
        jwt_issuer="https://auth.example.com",
        jwt_audience="api.example.com",
        jwt_public_key_pem="dummy",
        database_url="postgresql+asyncpg://x:x@localhost/x",
        redis_url="redis://localhost",
        rate_limit_requests=5,
        rate_limit_window_seconds=60,
        rate_limit_burst=0,
    )


class TestRateLimit:
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_allows_under_limit(self, settings):
        with patch("app.middleware.rate_limit._get_redis") as mock_redis_fn:
            redis = AsyncMock()
            redis.eval = AsyncMock(return_value=[1, 60, 1])  # count=1, allowed=1
            mock_redis_fn.return_value = redis

            req = _make_request(user_id="user_123")
            await check_rate_limit(req, settings)   # should not raise

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_raises_429_over_limit(self, settings):
        with patch("app.middleware.rate_limit._get_redis") as mock_redis_fn:
            redis = AsyncMock()
            redis.eval = AsyncMock(return_value=[6, 13, 0])  # denied
            mock_redis_fn.return_value = redis

            req = _make_request(user_id="user_123")
            with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
                await check_rate_limit(req, settings)
            assert exc.value.status_code == 429
            assert "Retry-After" in exc.value.headers

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_redis_failure_fails_open_with_warning(self, settings):
        """Redis down → fail open (rate limiting is primary at gateway)."""
        with patch("app.middleware.rate_limit._get_redis") as mock_redis_fn:
            redis = AsyncMock()
            redis.eval = AsyncMock(side_effect=ConnectionError("redis down"))
            mock_redis_fn.return_value = redis

            req = _make_request(user_id="user_123")
            await check_rate_limit(req, settings)   # must not raise

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_rate_limit_headers_set_on_request_state(self, settings):
        with patch("app.middleware.rate_limit._get_redis") as mock_redis_fn:
            redis = AsyncMock()
            redis.eval = AsyncMock(return_value=[3, 60, 1])
            mock_redis_fn.return_value = redis

            req = _make_request(user_id="user_123")
            await check_rate_limit(req, settings)
            assert hasattr(req.state, "rate_limit_headers")
            assert "RateLimit-Policy" in req.state.rate_limit_headers
```
